    95→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    96→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
    97→  end
    98→
    99→  # Public method for job usage
   100→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   101→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   102→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   103→
   104→    if sandbox.mount_home
   105→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   106→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   107→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   108→    end
   109→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   110→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   111→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   112→
   113→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   114→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   115→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   116→    end
   117→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   118→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(sandbox.volume_path)
   119→      prepare_bind_mount(sandbox.volume_path)
   120→    end
   121→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   122→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   123→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   124→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   125→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   126→    end
   127→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   128→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   129→  end
   130→
   131→  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false, archive: false)
   132→    begin
   133→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   134→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   135→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   136→    end
   137→
   138→    begin
   139→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   140→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   141→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   142→    end
   143→
   144→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   145→
   146→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   147→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   148→    end
   149→
   150→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   151→      begin
   152→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   153→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   154→        container.delete(force: true)
   155→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   156→        # Container already gone
   157→      end
   158→    end
   159→
   160→    if archive
   161→      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived
   162→      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current)
   163→    else
   164→      unless keep_volume
   165→        FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   166→      end
   167→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   168→    end
   169→  end
   170→
   171→  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
   172→  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
   173→  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
   174→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"
   175→
   176→    user = sandbox.user
   177→
   178→    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
   179→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   180→
   181→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   182→    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil)
   183→
   184→    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   185→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   186→    end
   187→
   188→    sandbox
   189→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   190→    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
   191→  end
   192→
   193→  def start(sandbox:)
   194→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   195→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   196→
   197→    user = sandbox.user
   198→
   199→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   200→      begin
   201→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   202→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   203→        old.delete(force: true)
   204→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   205→        # already gone
   206→      end
   207→    end
   208→
   209→    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
   210→    # Sysbox user-namespace UID remapping means the home/data dirs (created by
   211→    # host root) appear owned by nobody (UID 65534) inside the container, so
   212→    # chown in the entrypoint fails silently.  Resetting to 777 here ensures
   213→    # the new container's entrypoint can write .ssh, .Xauthority, etc.
   214→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   215→
   216→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   217→
   218→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   219→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   220→
   221→    sandbox
   222→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   223→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   224→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   225→  end
   226→
   227→  def stop(sandbox:)
   228→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   229→
   230→    begin
   231→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   232→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   233→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   234→    end
   235→
   236→    begin
   237→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   238→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   239→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   240→    end
   241→
   242→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   243→
   244→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   245→      begin
   246→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   247→        container.stop(t: 10)
   248→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   249→        # already gone
   250→      end
   251→    end
   252→
   253→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   254→    sandbox
   255→  end
   256→
   257→  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
   258→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   259→
   260→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   261→    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
   262→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   263→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   264→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   265→    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
   266→  end
   267→
   268→  def status(sandbox:)
   269→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   270→
   271→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   272→    info = container.json
   273→    {
   274→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   275→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   276→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   277→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   278→    }
   279→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   280→    { state: "not_found" }
   281→  end
   282→
   283→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   284→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   285→  #
   286→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
   287→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   288→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   289→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   290→
   291→    user = sandbox.user
   292→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   293→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]
   294→
   295→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   296→      user: user,
   297→      name: name,
   298→      label: label,
   299→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   300→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   301→    )
   302→
   303→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   304→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   305→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   306→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   307→      image = container.commit(
   308→        repo: repo,
   309→        tag: name,
   310→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   311→      )
   312→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   313→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   314→    end
   315→
   316→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   317→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   318→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   319→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   320→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   321→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   322→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   323→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   324→      end
   325→    end
   326→
   327→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   328→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   329→      if data_subdir.present?
   330→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   331→      else
   332→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   333→      end
   334→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   335→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   336→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   337→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   338→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   339→      end
   340→    end
   341→
   342→    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   343→    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   344→      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
   345→      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
   346→      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
   347→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
   348→        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
   349→        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
   350→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
   351→      end
   352→    end
   353→
   354→    snap.save!
   355→    snap
   356→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   357→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   358→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   359→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   360→  end
   361→
   362→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   363→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   364→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   365→    {
   366→      name: snap.name,
   367→      image: snap.docker_image,
   368→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   369→      created_at: snap.created_at
   370→    }
   371→  end
   372→
   373→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   374→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   375→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   376→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   377→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   378→  end
   379→
   380→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   381→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   382→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   383→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   384→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   385→  end
   386→
   387→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   388→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   389→
   390→    if snap
   391→      # Remove Docker image
   392→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   393→        begin
   394→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   395→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   396→          # Already gone
   397→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   398→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   399→        end
   400→      end
   401→
   402→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   403→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   404→        begin
   405→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   406→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   407→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   408→        end
   409→      end
   410→
   411→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   412→        begin
   413→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   414→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   415→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   416→        end
   417→      end
   418→
   419→      snap.destroy!
   420→    else
   421→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   422→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   423→      begin
   424→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   425→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   426→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   427→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   428→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   429→      end
   430→    end
   431→  end
   432→
   433→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   434→    user = sandbox.user
   435→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   436→
   437→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   438→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   439→
   440→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   441→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   442→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   443→    else
   444→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   445→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   446→    end
   447→
   448→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   449→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   450→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   451→
   452→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   453→    if restore_container
   454→      begin
   455→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   456→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   457→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   458→      end
   459→    end
   460→
   461→    begin
   462→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   463→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   464→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   465→    end
   466→
   467→    begin
   468→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   469→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   470→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   471→    end
   472→
   473→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   474→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   475→    end
   476→
   477→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   478→      begin
   479→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   480→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   481→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   482→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   483→        # Already gone
   484→      end
   485→    end
   486→
   487→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   488→    if restore_home
   489→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   490→      begin
   491→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   492→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   493→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   494→      end
   495→    end
   496→
   497→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   498→    if restore_data
   499→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   500→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   501→      else
   502→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   503→      end
   504→      begin
   505→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   506→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   507→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   508→      end
   509→    end
   510→
   511→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   512→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   513→
   514→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   515→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   516→      "Image" => final_image,
   517→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   518→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   519→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
   520→      "HostConfig" => {
   521→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   522→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
   523→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   524→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   525→      },
   526→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
   527→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
   528→      }
   529→    )
   530→
   531→    container.start
   532→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   533→
   534→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
   535→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
   536→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
   537→
   538→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   539→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   540→    end
   541→
   542→    sandbox
   543→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   544→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   545→  end
   546→
   547→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   548→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   549→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   550→
   551→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   552→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   553→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   554→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   555→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   556→        next if tag_name.blank?
   557→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   558→
   559→        Snapshot.create!(
   560→          user: user,
   561→          name: tag_name,
   562→          docker_image: tag,
   563→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   564→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   565→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   566→        )
   567→      end
   568→    end
   569→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   570→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   571→  end
   572→
   573→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   574→    {
   575→      name: snap.name,
   576→      label: snap.label,
   577→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   578→      layers: snap.layers,
   579→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   580→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   581→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   582→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   583→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   584→      created_at: snap.created_at
   585→    }
   586→  end
   587→
   588→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   589→    user = sandbox.user.name
   590→
   591→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   592→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   593→    end
   594→
   595→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   596→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   597→
   598→    {
   599→      host: ts_ip,
   600→      port: 22,
   601→      user: user,
   602→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   603→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   604→    }
   605→  end
   606→
   607→  private
   608→
   609→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   610→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   611→    max_attempts.times do
   612→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   613→
   614→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   615→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   616→        sleep delay
   617→        next
   618→      end
   619→
   620→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   621→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   622→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   623→
   624→      sleep delay
   625→    end
   626→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   627→    nil
   628→  end
   629→
   630→  def connect_to_network(container)
   631→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   632→    network.connect(container.id)
   633→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   634→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   635→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   636→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   637→  end
   638→
   639→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   640→    env = [
   641→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   642→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   643→    ]
   644→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   645→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   646→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   647→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   648→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   649→    env
   650→  end
   651→
   652→  def container_runtime
   653→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   654→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   655→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   656→        "sysbox-runc"
   657→      else
   658→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   659→        "runc"
   660→      end
   661→    end
   662→  end
   663→
   664→  # Prepare a directory for bind-mounting into Sysbox containers.
   665→  # Sets 777 mode and root ownership. On kernel 6.17+, Sysbox cannot create
   666→  # entries inside bind-mounted dirs owned by UIDs outside the container's
   667→  # user-namespace mapping (even with 777 mode). Host root (UID 0) is always
   668→  # mapped inside Sysbox. Uses sudo because the directory may already be owned
   669→  # by a Sysbox-remapped UID from a previous container run.
   670→  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
   671→    stat = File.stat(path)
   672→    system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path) unless stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
   673→    system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chown", "0:0", path) unless stat.uid == 0
   674→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   675→    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
   676→  end
   677→
   678→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   679→    binds = []
   680→    if sandbox.mount_home
   681→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   682→    end
   683→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   684→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   685→    end
   686→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   687→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   688→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   689→    end
   690→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   691→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   692→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   693→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   694→    end
   695→    binds
   696→  end
   697→end
   698→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sandcastle installer
     3→# Usage: installer.sh [gen-env|install|reset|uninstall|help]
     4→set -euo pipefail
     5→
     6→# ═══ Colors & helpers ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     7→
     8→RED='\033[0;31m'
     9→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    11→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    12→NC='\033[0m'
    13→
    14→info()  { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    15→ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*" >&2; }
    18→die()   { error "$@"; exit 1; }
    19→
    20→WRITTEN_FILES=()
    21→wrote() { WRITTEN_FILES+=("$1"); }
    22→print_written_files() {
    23→  if [ ${#WRITTEN_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    24→    echo ""
    25→    info "Files created/updated:"
    26→    for f in "${WRITTEN_FILES[@]}"; do
    27→      echo -e "    ${f}"
    28→    done
    29→  fi
    30→  WRITTEN_FILES=()
    31→}
    32→
    33→require_root() {
    34→  [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ] || die "This command must be run as root (use sudo)"
    35→}
    36→
    37→# ═══ Parse command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    38→
    39→COMMAND="${1:-install}"
    40→shift 2>/dev/null || true
    41→
    42→case "$COMMAND" in
    43→  gen-env|install|update|reset|uninstall) ;;
    44→  help|-h|--help) COMMAND="help" ;;
    45→  *) die "Unknown command: $COMMAND (use 'help' for usage)" ;;
    46→esac
    47→
    48→# ═══ Help ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→if [ "$COMMAND" = "help" ]; then
    51→  cat <<'USAGE'
    52→Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
    53→
    54→Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
    55→Sandcastle platform.
    56→
    57→Commands:
    58→  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
    59→  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
    60→  update       Pull latest images and restart services
    61→  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
    62→  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
    63→  help         Show this help message
    64→
    65→install options:
    66→  --from-backup <file>   Restore data from a backup file after installing.
    67→                         Secrets, database, and user data are loaded from the
    68→                         backup instead of being generated fresh.
    69→                         SANDCASTLE_HOST is still required in sandcastle.env.
    70→                         SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD are not required.
    71→
    72→Environment:
    73→  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
    74→                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
    75→                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)
    76→
    77→Workflow (fresh install):
    78→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
    79→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
    80→  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
    81→
    82→Workflow (restore from backup):
    83→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config (set SANDCASTLE_HOST)
    84→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # set host, TLS mode — no admin password needed
    85→  3. sudo installer.sh install --from-backup ./sandcastle-backup-*.tar.zst
    86→USAGE
    87→  exit 0
    88→fi
    89→
    90→# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    91→
    92→load_env() {
    93→  local script_dir
    94→  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    95→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
    96→
    97→  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
    98→  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    99→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
   100→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
   101→  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
   102→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
   103→  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   104→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
   105→  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   106→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
   107→  fi
   108→
   109→  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
   110→    set -a
   111→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   112→    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   113→    set +a
   114→    info "Loaded $LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   115→  fi
   116→}
   117→
   118→# ═══ derive_vars ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   119→
   120→derive_vars() {
   121→  SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
   122→  APP_IMAGE="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
   123→  SANDBOX_IMAGE="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
   124→  SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
   125→  SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$SANDCASTLE_USER}"
   126→  SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
   127→  SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
   128→  SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
   129→  SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
   130→  TCP_PORT_MIN="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}"
   131→  TCP_PORT_MAX="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
   132→  SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
   133→  SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"
   134→
   135→  DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$SANDCASTLE_HOME}"
   136→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
   137→
   138→  # Single RFC 1918 /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   139→  # Must be a private range (10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x).
   140→  SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-10.89.0.0/16}"
   141→  _priv_base="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET%%/*}"        # e.g. "10.89.0.0"
   142→  _priv_prefix="${_priv_base%.*.*}"                  # e.g. "10.89"
   143→  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.1/24}"
   144→  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.0/24}"
   145→  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}}"
   146→  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   147→
   148→  DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker.sock"
   149→  DOCKER="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime/bin/docker"
   150→
   151→  ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture 2>/dev/null || echo "amd64")
   152→
   153→  # Resolve password from file if needed
   154→  if [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE:-}" ]; then
   155→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE" ] && die "Password file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE"
   156→    SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD="$(cat "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE")"
   157→  fi
   158→}
   159→
   160→# ═══ setup_ssh_keys ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   161→# Copy deploying user's SSH keys to sandcastle user
   162→
   163→setup_ssh_keys() {
   164→  local deploying_user="${SUDO_USER:-}"
   165→
   166→  if [ -z "$deploying_user" ]; then
   167→    warn "No SUDO_USER found — skipping SSH key setup"
   168→    return
   169→  fi
   170→
   171→  local source_keys="/home/${deploying_user}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
   172→  local target_dir="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.ssh"
   173→  local target_keys="${target_dir}/authorized_keys"
   174→
   175→  if [ ! -f "$source_keys" ]; then
   176→    warn "No SSH keys found for user '${deploying_user}' — skipping SSH key setup"
   177→    return
   178→  fi
   179→
   180→  info "Setting up SSH keys for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'..."
   181→
   182→  # Create .ssh directory if it doesn't exist
   183→  mkdir -p "$target_dir"
   184→  chmod 700 "$target_dir"
   185→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$target_dir"
   186→
   187→  # Copy authorized_keys
   188→  cp "$source_keys" "$target_keys"
   189→  chmod 600 "$target_keys"
   190→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$target_keys"
   191→
   192→  ok "SSH keys configured for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'"
   193→}
   194→
   195→# ═══ setup_passwordless_sudo ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   196→# Add sandcastle user to sudoers with NOPASSWD:ALL
   197→
   198→setup_passwordless_sudo() {
   199→  local sudoers_file="/etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle"
   200→

195→# ═══ setup_passwordless_sudo ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   196→# Add sandcastle user to sudoers with NOPASSWD:ALL
   197→
   198→setup_passwordless_sudo() {
   199→  local sudoers_file="/etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle"
   200→
   201→  info "Configuring passwordless sudo for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'..."
   202→
   203→  echo "${SANDCASTLE_USER} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > "$sudoers_file"
   204→  chmod 440 "$sudoers_file"
   205→
   206→  # Validate sudoers syntax
   207→  if ! visudo -cf "$sudoers_file" &>/dev/null; then
   208→    rm -f "$sudoers_file"
   209→    warn "Failed to validate sudoers file — skipping passwordless sudo setup"
   210→    return
   211→  fi
   212→
   213→  wrote "$sudoers_file"
   214→  ok "Passwordless sudo configured for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'"
   215→}
   216→
   217→# ═══ setup_bashrc_path ════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   218→# Add docker-runtime/bin to PATH in .profile and .bashrc (idempotent)
   219→
   220→setup_bashrc_path() {
   221→  local profile="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.profile"
   222→  local bashrc="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.bashrc"
   223→  local path_export="export PATH=${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/docker-runtime/bin:${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/bin:\$PATH"
   224→
   225→  info "Configuring PATH in .profile and .bashrc..."
   226→
   227→  # Add to .profile for SSH non-interactive shells
   228→  touch "$profile"
   229→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$profile"
   230→
   231→  if ! grep -qF "$path_export" "$profile" 2>/dev/null; then
   232→    echo "$path_export" >> "$profile"
   233→    wrote "$profile"
   234→  fi
   235→
   236→  # Add to .bashrc for interactive shells
   237→  touch "$bashrc"
   238→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$bashrc"
   239→
   240→  if ! grep -qF "$path_export" "$bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
   241→    echo "$path_export" >> "$bashrc"
   242→    wrote "$bashrc"
   243→  fi
   244→
   245→  ok "PATH configured in .profile and .bashrc"
   246→}
   247→
   248→# ═══ install_prerequisites ════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   249→# Install required host tools: zstd (for backup/restore), rsync (for data copy)
   250→
   251→install_prerequisites() {
   252→  local missing=()
   253→  command -v zstd >/dev/null 2>&1  || missing+=("zstd")
   254→  command -v rsync >/dev/null 2>&1 || missing+=("rsync")
   255→  if [ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
   256→    info "Installing prerequisites: ${missing[*]}..."
   257→    apt-get install -y "${missing[@]}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
   258→      || warn "Failed to install ${missing[*]} — backup/restore may not work"
   259→    ok "Prerequisites installed: ${missing[*]}"
   260→  fi
   261→}
   262→
   263→# ═══ write_admin_script ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   264→# Install sandcastle-admin to $SANDCASTLE_HOME/bin/
   265→
   266→write_admin_script() {
   267→  local bin_dir="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/bin"
   268→  mkdir -p "$bin_dir"
   269→
   270→  cat > "$bin_dir/sandcastle-admin" <<'__ADMIN_EOF__'
   271→@@TEMPLATE:templates/sandcastle-admin.sh@@
   272→__ADMIN_EOF__
   273→
   274→  chmod +x "$bin_dir/sandcastle-admin"
   275→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$bin_dir/sandcastle-admin"
   276→  wrote "$bin_dir/sandcastle-admin"
   277→  ok "sandcastle-admin installed to $bin_dir"
   278→}
   279→
   280→# ═══ setup_login_banner ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
   281→# Create a login banner showing Sandcastle version and info
   282→
   283→setup_login_banner() {
   284→  local profile_d="/etc/profile.d/sandcastle-banner.sh"
   285→
   286→  info "Setting up login banner..."
   287→
   288→  cat > "$profile_d" <<'BANNER_SCRIPT'
   289→@@TEMPLATE:banner.sh@@
   290→BANNER_SCRIPT
   291→
   292→  chmod +x "$profile_d"
   293→  wrote "$profile_d"
   294→  ok "Login banner configured"
   295→}
   296→
   297→# ═══ ensure_dirs ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   298→# Create/fix data directories and ownership. Safe to run repeatedly.
   299→
   300→ensure_dirs() {
   301→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/etc
   302→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/bin
   303→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/{users,sandboxes,wetty,postgres,snapshots}
   304→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/traefik/{dynamic,certs}
   305→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_USER}:${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}" "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"
   306→  # Own top-level data dirs (not -R: per-user subdirs are bind-mounted into
   307→  # Sysbox containers which use a different UID range via /etc/subuid).
   308→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" \
   309→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users \
   310→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/sandboxes \
   311→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/snapshots \
   312→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/wetty
   313→  chown -R "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" \
   314→    "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/traefik/dynamic
   315→  # Per-user dirs: own the user-level parent, then chmod 777 the bind-mount
   316→  # targets so Sysbox-mapped root can write to them.
   317→  for d in "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users/*; do
   318→    [ -d "$d" ] && chown "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" "$d"
   319→  done
   320→  for d in "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users/*/home \
   321→           "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/users/*/data \
   322→           "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/sandboxes/*/vol; do
   323→    [ -d "$d" ] && chmod 777 "$d"
   324→  done
   325→  # Fix home dir permissions inside running sandbox containers so sshd
   326→  # StrictModes is satisfied (rejects 777 home dirs).
   327→  for cid in $($DOCKER ps -q 2>/dev/null); do
   328→    user=$($DOCKER inspect --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' "$cid" \
   329→      | grep '^SANDCASTLE_USER=' | cut -d= -f2 || true)
   330→    [ -n "$user" ] && $DOCKER exec "$cid" chmod 755 "/home/$user" 2>/dev/null || true
   331→  done
   332→  usermod -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME" "$SANDCASTLE_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
   333→  ok "Data directories verified"
   334→}
   335→
   336→# ═══ Helpers ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   337→
   338→show_image_info() {
   339→  local repo="$1"
   340→  python3 -c "
   341→import urllib.request, json, sys, re
   342→from datetime import datetime, timezone
   343→repo, arch = '${repo}', '${ARCH}'
   344→try:
   345→    r = urllib.request.urlopen(f'https://ghcr.io/token?scope=repository:thieso2/{repo}:pull')
   346→    token = json.load(r)['token']
   347→    h = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
   348→    req = urllib.request.Request(
   349→        f'https://ghcr.io/v2/thieso2/{repo}/manifests/latest',
   350→        headers={**h, 'Accept': 'application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json'})
   351→    m = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
   352→    if 'manifests' in m:
   353→        for p in m['manifests']:
   354→            if p.get('platform', {}).get('architecture') == arch:
   355→                req = urllib.request.Request(
   356→                    f'https://ghcr.io/v2/thieso2/{repo}/manifests/{p[\"digest\"]}',
   357→                    headers={**h, 'Accept': 'application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json'})
   358→                m = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
   359→                break
   360→    cfg = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(
   361→        urllib.request.Request(f'https://ghcr.io/v2/thieso2/{repo}/blobs/{m[\"config\"][\"digest\"]}', headers=h)))
   362→    ts = re.sub(r'(\.\d{6})\d+', r'\1', cfg['created']).replace('Z', '+00:00')
   363→    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(ts)
   364→    secs = int((datetime.now(timezone.utc) - dt).total_seconds())
   365→    if secs < 0: secs = 0
   366→    if secs >= 86400: ago = f'{secs // 86400}d ago'
   367→    elif secs >= 3600: ago = f'{secs // 3600}h ago'
   368→    else: ago = f'{max(1, secs // 60)}m ago'
   369→    print(f'  ghcr.io/thieso2/{repo}:latest    built {dt.strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC\")} ({ago})')
   370→except Exception:
   371→    print(f'  ghcr.io/thieso2/{repo}:latest    (unable to fetch build info)')
   372→" 2>/dev/null || echo "  ghcr.io/thieso2/${repo}:latest"
   373→}
   374→
   375→pick_private_net() {
   376→  # Pick a random 10.X.0.0/16 not already routed on this host.
   377→  local used_octets
   378→  used_octets=$(
   379→    { ip route 2>/dev/null; ip addr 2>/dev/null; } \
   380→      | grep -oE '10\.[0-9]+\.' \
   381→      | grep -oE '\.[0-9]+\.' \
   382→      | tr -d '.' \
   383→      | sort -un
   384→  )
   385→  for b in $(shuf -i 1-254 2>/dev/null || python3 -c "import random,sys; l=list(range(1,255)); random.shuffle(l); print('\n'.join(map(str,l)))"); do
   386→    if ! echo "$used_octets" | grep -qx "$b"; then
   387→      echo "10.${b}.0.0/16"
   388→      return
   389→    fi
   390→  done
   391→  # Fallback
   392→  echo "10.$(shuf -i 1-254 -n1 2>/dev/null || echo 89).0.0/16"
   393→}
   394→
   395→find_free_subnet() {
   396→  local used
   397→  used=$(
   398→    { ip route 2>/dev/null; ip addr 2>/dev/null; netstat -rn 2>/dev/null; } \
   399→      | grep -oE '(10|172|192)\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
   400→      | sort -un
   401→    $DOCKER network ls -q 2>/dev/null | while read -r nid; do
   402→      $DOCKER network inspect --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' "$nid" 2>/dev/null
   403→    done | grep -oE '(10|172|192)\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' \
   404→      | sort -un
   405→  )
   406→  for b in $(shuf -i 16-31); do
   407→    for c in $(shuf -i 1-254 | head -5); do
   408→      if ! echo "$used" | grep -q "^172\.${b}\.${c}\."; then
   409→        echo "172.${b}.${c}.0/24"
   410→        return
   411→      fi
   412→    done
   413→  done
   414→  for b in $(shuf -i 1-254 | head -20); do
   415→    for c in $(shuf -i 1-254 | head -5); do
   416→      if ! echo "$used" | grep -q "^10\.${b}\.${c}\."; then
   417→        echo "10.${b}.${c}.0/24"
   418→        return
   419→      fi
   420→    done
   421→  done
   422→  echo "172.30.99.0/24"
   423→}
   424→
   425→install_mkcert() {
   426→  if command -v mkcert &>/dev/null; then
   427→    return 0
   428→  fi
   429→  info "Installing mkcert..."
   430→  local arch
   431→  arch=$(uname -m)
   432→  local mkcert_arch
   433→  case "$arch" in
   434→    x86_64)  mkcert_arch="amd64" ;;
   435→    aarch64) mkcert_arch="arm64" ;;
   436→    *)       die "Unsupported architecture for mkcert: $arch" ;;
   437→  esac
   438→  curl -fsSL "https://dl.filippo.io/mkcert/latest?for=linux/${mkcert_arch}" \
   439→    -o /usr/local/bin/mkcert
   440→  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/mkcert
   441→  ok "mkcert installed"
   442→}
   443→
   444→# ═══ write_compose ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   445→
   446→# Write the bundled dockyard.sh to /tmp so it can be invoked without wget.
   447→write_dockyard_sh() {
   448→  cat > /tmp/dockyard.sh <<'__DOCKYARD_BUNDLED_EOF__'
   449→@@TEMPLATE:templates/dockyard.sh@@
   450→__DOCKYARD_BUNDLED_EOF__
   451→  chmod +x /tmp/dockyard.sh
   452→}
   453→
   454→write_helper_scripts() {
   455→  cat > "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime/bin/docker-logs" <<LOGS
   456→#!/bin/bash
   457→exec sudo ${DOCKER} compose -f ${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/docker-compose.yml logs -f "\$@"
   458→LOGS
   459→  chmod +x "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime/bin/docker-logs"
   460→  wrote "${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime/bin/docker-logs"
   461→}
   462→
   463→write_compose() {
   464→  local DATA_MOUNT="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data"
   465→
   466→  cat > "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/docker-compose.yml" <<COMPOSE
   467→@@TEMPLATE:docker-compose.yml.template@@
   468→COMPOSE
   469→
   470→  wrote "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/docker-compose.yml"
   471→}
   472→
   473→# ═══ cmd_gen_env ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   474→
   475→cmd_gen_env() {
   476→  local out="${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-./sandcastle.env}"
   477→  [ -f "$out" ] && die "File already exists: $out"
   478→
   479→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
   480→  local app_image="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
   481→  local sandbox_image="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
   482→  local user="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
   483→  local group="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$user}"
   484→  local uid="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
   485→  local gid="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
   486→  local http_port="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
   487→  local https_port="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
   488→  local tls_mode="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
   489→
   490→  # Auto-detect host IP
   491→  local host="${SANDCASTLE_HOST:-}"
   492→  if [ -z "$host" ]; then
   493→    host=$(hostname -I 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.' | head -1 || true)
   494→    host="${host:-$(curl -4fsSL --max-time 5 https://ifconfig.me 2>/dev/null || true)}"

500→
   501→  local dy_root="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$home}"
   502→  local dy_prefix="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
   503→  local priv_net="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-$(pick_private_net)}"
   504→  local _pb="${priv_net%%/*}"; local _pp="${_pb%.*.*}"
   505→  local dy_bridge="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_pp}.0.1/24}"
   506→  local dy_fixed="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_pp}.0.0/24}"
   507→  local dy_pool="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${priv_net}}"
   508→  local dy_pool_size="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   509→
   510→  # Auto-derive server name from short hostname (used for Tailscale machine names)
   511→  local sc_name="${SANDCASTLE_NAME:-$(hostname -s 2>/dev/null || true)}"
   512→  sc_name="${sc_name:-sandcastle}"
   513→
   514→  cat > "$out" <<EOF
   515→# Sandcastle configuration
   516→# Edit values below, then run: sudo installer.sh install
   517→
   518→# ─── Paths & images ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   519→SANDCASTLE_HOME=${home}
   520→APP_IMAGE=${app_image}
   521→SANDBOX_IMAGE=${sandbox_image}
   522→
   523→# ─── System user (home=SANDCASTLE_HOME, shell=/bin/bash) ────────────────────
   524→SANDCASTLE_USER=${user}
   525→SANDCASTLE_GROUP=${group}
   526→SANDCASTLE_UID=${uid}
   527→SANDCASTLE_GID=${gid}
   528→
   529→# ─── Server identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   530→# Human-readable name for this Sandcastle instance.
   531→# Used as the Tailscale machine name for sidecar containers: sc-<name>
   532→# (spaces and special chars are slugified automatically).
   533→SANDCASTLE_NAME=${sc_name}
   534→
   535→# ─── Network & TLS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   536→SANDCASTLE_HOST=${host}
   537→SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE=${tls_mode}
   538→#ACME_EMAIL=admin@example.com
   539→SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT=${http_port}
   540→SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT=${https_port}
   541→
   542→# ─── Admin account (required for fresh install) ─────────────────────────────
   543→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER=${admin_user}
   544→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL=${admin_email}
   545→#SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD=changeme
   546→#REDACTED
   547→#SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY=ssh-ed25519 AAAA...
   548→
   549→# ─── OAuth (optional — enables "Sign in with …" buttons) ──────────────────
   550→#GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=
   551→#GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=
   552→#GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=
   553→#GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=
   554→
   555→# ─── Dockyard (Docker + Sysbox) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   556→DOCKYARD_ROOT=${dy_root}
   557→DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=${dy_prefix}
   558→
   559→# Private /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   560→# Must be RFC 1918: 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x.
   561→# Bridge and pool CIDRs are derived from this (override individually below if needed).
   562→SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET=${priv_net}
   563→#DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=${dy_bridge}
   564→#DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=${dy_fixed}
   565→#DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${dy_pool}
   566→DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=${dy_pool_size}
   567→EOF
   568→
   569→  ok "Generated $out — edit it, then run: sudo installer.sh install"
   570→}
   571→
   572→# ═══ cmd_destroy ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   573→
   574→cmd_destroy() {
   575→  local auto_confirm="${1:-false}"
   576→
   577→  require_root
   578→
   579→  # Load installed env for CREATED_USER/CREATED_GROUP
   580→  if [ -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   581→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   582→    source "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env"
   583→  fi
   584→
   585→  warn "This will remove Sandcastle from $SANDCASTLE_HOME (containers, config, services)"
   586→  if [ "$auto_confirm" != "true" ]; then
   587→    read -rp "Are you sure? (yes to confirm): " CONFIRM
   588→    [ "$CONFIRM" = "yes" ] || die "Aborted"
   589→  fi
   590→
   591→  info "Tearing down Sandcastle..."
   592→  cd /
   593→
   594→  if [ -x "$DOCKER" ]; then
   595→    for container in $($DOCKER ps -a --filter "name=^sc-ts-" --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null); do
   596→      info "Removing Tailscale sidecar: $container"
   597→      $DOCKER rm -f "$container" 2>/dev/null || true
   598→    done
   599→    for container in $($DOCKER ps -a --filter "runtime=sysbox-runc" --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null); do
   600→      info "Removing container: $container"
   601→      $DOCKER rm -f "$container" 2>/dev/null || true
   602→    done
   603→    for net in $($DOCKER network ls --filter "name=^sc-ts-net-" --format '{{.Name}}' 2>/dev/null); do
   604→      info "Removing network: $net"
   605→      $DOCKER network rm "$net" 2>/dev/null || true
   606→    done
   607→    if [ -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/docker-compose.yml" ]; then
   608→      # Do NOT pass --volumes: postgres data lives in a bind-mount under
   609→      # $SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres which must survive uninstall.
   610→      $DOCKER compose -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/docker-compose.yml" down --rmi all --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
   611→    fi
   612→    $DOCKER network rm sandcastle-web 2>/dev/null || true
   613→
   614→    # Remove sandbox image explicitly (not removed by compose down)
   615→    if [ -n "${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-}" ]; then
   616→      info "Removing sandbox image: $SANDBOX_IMAGE"
   617→      $DOCKER rmi "$SANDBOX_IMAGE" 2>/dev/null || true
   618→    fi
   619→  fi
   620→
   621→  # Destroy Dockyard
   622→  DOCKYARD_ENV_FILE="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/dockyard.env"
   623→  if [ -f "$DOCKYARD_ENV_FILE" ] || systemctl cat "${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker.service" &>/dev/null; then
   624→    info "Destroying Dockyard..."
   625→    if [ -f "$DOCKYARD_ENV_FILE" ] && write_dockyard_sh 2>/dev/null; then
   626→      echo "y" | DOCKYARD_ENV="$DOCKYARD_ENV_FILE" bash /tmp/dockyard.sh destroy >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   627→      rm -f /tmp/dockyard.sh
   628→    else
   629→      systemctl stop "${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker" 2>/dev/null || true
   630→      systemctl disable "${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker" 2>/dev/null || true
   631→      rm -f "/etc/systemd/system/${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker.service"
   632→      systemctl daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
   633→      ip link delete "${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker0" 2>/dev/null || true
   634→    fi
   635→    ok "Dockyard destroyed"
   636→  fi
   637→
   638→  # Remove user and group
   639→  if id "$SANDCASTLE_USER" &>/dev/null; then
   640→    # Do NOT use -r: the user's home ($SANDCASTLE_HOME) contains data that
   641→    # must be preserved across reinstalls. Directories are cleaned up explicitly below.
   642→    userdel "$SANDCASTLE_USER" 2>/dev/null || true
   643→    ok "Removed user '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'"
   644→  fi
   645→  if getent group "$SANDCASTLE_GROUP" &>/dev/null; then
   646→    groupdel "$SANDCASTLE_GROUP" 2>/dev/null || true
   647→    ok "Removed group '${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}'"
   648→  fi
   649→
   650→  # sandcastle-nat.service no longer used (dockyard handles NAT for user-defined networks)
   651→
   652→  # Revert UFW firewall rules
   653→  if command -v ufw &>/dev/null && ufw status 2>/dev/null | grep -q "Status: active"; then
   654→    info "Reverting firewall rules..."
   655→    ufw delete allow "${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}/tcp" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   656→    ufw delete allow "${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}/tcp" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   657→    ufw delete allow 2201:2299/tcp >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   658→    ok "Firewall rules reverted"
   659→  fi
   660→
   661→  # Remove sudoers file
   662→  if [ -f /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle ]; then
   663→    rm -f /etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle
   664→    ok "Removed sudoers file"
   665→  fi
   666→
   667→  # Remove login banner
   668→  if [ -f "/etc/profile.d/sandcastle-banner.sh" ]; then
   669→    rm -f /etc/profile.d/sandcastle-banner.sh
   670→    ok "Removed login banner"
   671→  fi
   672→
   673→  # Remove Sandcastle files — keep user data (data/users, data/sandboxes, data/postgres)
   674→  # Preserve data/traefik/acme.json (Let's Encrypt certs) to survive reinstalls.
   675→  rm -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   676→  rm -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/docker-compose.yml"
   677→  rm -rf "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc"
   678→  rm -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik/traefik.yml"
   679→  rm -rf "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik/dynamic"
   680→  rm -rf "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik/certs"
   681→  rmdir "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik" 2>/dev/null || true
   682→  rmdir "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data" 2>/dev/null || true
   683→  rmdir "$SANDCASTLE_HOME" 2>/dev/null || true
   684→  rm -rf "/run/${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker"
   685→
   686→  if [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/users" ] || [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/sandboxes" ] || [ -d "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres" ]; then
   687→    warn "User data preserved in $SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/ — remove manually if no longer needed"
   688→  fi
   689→  # Legacy: older installs stored postgres in a Docker named volume 'pgdata'
   690→  if $DOCKER volume inspect pgdata &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
   691→    warn "Legacy Docker volume 'pgdata' still exists — run '$DOCKER volume rm pgdata' to delete it"
   692→  fi
   693→
   694→  ok "Sandcastle destroyed"
   695→}
   696→
   697→# ═══ cmd_install ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   698→
   699→cmd_install() {
   700→  # Parse install-specific options
   701→  local BACKUP_FILE=""
   702→  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
   703→    case "$1" in
   704→      --from-backup)
   705→        BACKUP_FILE="$2"
   706→        [ -n "$BACKUP_FILE" ] || die "--from-backup requires a backup file path"
   707→        [ -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ] || die "Backup file not found: $BACKUP_FILE"
   708→        shift 2
   709→        ;;
   710→      *)
   711→        die "Unknown option for install: $1 (use --from-backup <file>)"
   712→        ;;
   713→    esac
   714→  done
   715→
   716→  require_root
   717→
   718→  if ! grep -qi ubuntu /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null; then
   719→    warn "This script is tested on Ubuntu 24.04. Other distros may work but are unsupported."
   720→  fi
   721→  if [ "$ARCH" != "amd64" ] && [ "$ARCH" != "arm64" ]; then
   722→    die "Sandcastle requires amd64 or arm64 architecture (got: $ARCH)"
   723→  fi
   724→
   725→  echo ""
   726→  echo -e "${BLUE}═══ Sandcastle Installer ═══${NC}"
   727→  echo ""
   728→  info "Available images (${ARCH}):"
   729→  show_image_info "sandcastle"
   730→  show_image_info "sandcastle-sandbox"
   731→  echo ""
   732→
   733→  # ── Install Dockyard ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   734→
   735→  if [ -S "$DOCKER_SOCK" ]; then
   736→    ok "Dockyard already installed"
   737→    # The docker CLI binary ships with compose built-in. A leftover standalone
   738→    # compose plugin in cli-plugins/ shadows the built-in and may segfault.
   739→    # Remove it so the built-in is used instead.
   740→    local _stale_plugin="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose"
   741→    if [ -f "$_stale_plugin" ]; then
   742→      rm -f "$_stale_plugin"
   743→      ok "Removed stale compose plugin (compose is now built into docker CLI)"
   744→    fi
   745→    "$DOCKER" compose version &>/dev/null || die "Docker Compose not available — reinstall Dockyard"
   746→  else
   747→    info "Installing Dockyard (Docker + Sysbox)..."
   748→    write_dockyard_sh
   749→
   750→    mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc"
   751→    cat > "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/dockyard.env" <<DYEOF
   752→DOCKYARD_ROOT=${DOCKYARD_ROOT}
   753→DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}
   754→DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR}
   755→DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR}
   756→DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE}
   757→DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE}
   758→DYEOF
   759→    wrote "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/dockyard.env"
   760→
   761→    DOCKYARD_ENV="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/dockyard.env" bash /tmp/dockyard.sh create
   762→    rm -f /tmp/dockyard.sh
   763→
   764→    for i in $(seq 1 30); do
   765→      [ -S "$DOCKER_SOCK" ] && break
   766→      sleep 1
   767→    done
   768→    [ -S "$DOCKER_SOCK" ] || die "Dockyard socket not found at $DOCKER_SOCK after 30s"
   769→    ok "Dockyard installed"
   770→  fi
   771→
   772→  # ── Configure UFW ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   773→
   774→  if command -v ufw &>/dev/null; then
   775→    info "Configuring firewall..."
   776→    ufw --force reset >/dev/null 2>&1
   777→    ufw default deny incoming >/dev/null 2>&1
   778→    ufw default allow outgoing >/dev/null 2>&1
   779→    ufw allow 22/tcp >/dev/null 2>&1
   780→    ufw allow "${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT}/tcp" >/dev/null 2>&1
   781→    ufw allow "${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT}/tcp" >/dev/null 2>&1
   782→    ufw allow 2201:2299/tcp >/dev/null 2>&1
   783→    ufw allow "${TCP_PORT_MIN}:${TCP_PORT_MAX}/tcp" >/dev/null 2>&1
   784→    ufw --force enable >/dev/null 2>&1
   785→    ok "Firewall configured (22, ${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT}, ${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT}, 2201-2299, ${TCP_PORT_MIN}-${TCP_PORT_MAX})"
   786→  else
   787→    warn "UFW not found — skipping firewall setup"
   788→  fi
   789→
   790→  # ── Create system user/group ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   791→
   792→  CREATED_GROUP=false
   793→  CREATED_USER=false
   794→
   795→  if getent group "$SANDCASTLE_GROUP" &>/dev/null; then
   796→    ok "Group '${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}' already exists"
   797→  else
   798→    groupadd --system --gid "$SANDCASTLE_GID" "$SANDCASTLE_GROUP"
   799→    CREATED_GROUP=true

795→  if getent group "$SANDCASTLE_GROUP" &>/dev/null; then
   796→    ok "Group '${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}' already exists"
   797→  else
   798→    groupadd --system --gid "$SANDCASTLE_GID" "$SANDCASTLE_GROUP"
   799→    CREATED_GROUP=true
   800→    ok "Created group '${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}' (GID ${SANDCASTLE_GID})"
   801→  fi
   802→
   803→  if id "$SANDCASTLE_USER" &>/dev/null; then
   804→    ok "User '${SANDCASTLE_USER}' already exists"
   805→  else
   806→    useradd --system --uid "$SANDCASTLE_UID" --gid "$SANDCASTLE_GID" \
   807→      --home-dir "$SANDCASTLE_HOME" --shell /bin/bash "$SANDCASTLE_USER"
   808→    CREATED_USER=true
   809→    ok "Created user '${SANDCASTLE_USER}' (UID ${SANDCASTLE_UID})"
   810→  fi
   811→
   812→  # Add sandcastle user to docker group for socket access
   813→  DOCKER_GROUP=$(stat -c '%G' "$DOCKER_SOCK" 2>/dev/null || echo "docker")
   814→  if getent group "$DOCKER_GROUP" &>/dev/null; then
   815→    usermod -aG "$DOCKER_GROUP" "$SANDCASTLE_USER"
   816→    ok "Added '${SANDCASTLE_USER}' to group '${DOCKER_GROUP}'"
   817→  fi
   818→
   819→  # ── Create directories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   820→
   821→  ensure_dirs
   822→
   823→  # ── SSH & sudo setup ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   824→
   825→  setup_ssh_keys
   826→  setup_passwordless_sudo
   827→  setup_bashrc_path
   828→  setup_login_banner
   829→
   830→  # ── Detect fresh install vs upgrade ─────────────────────────────────────
   831→
   832→  FRESH_INSTALL=false
   833→  [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" ] && FRESH_INSTALL=true
   834→
   835→  # ── Fresh install: validate & generate .env ─────────────────────────────
   836→
   837→  if [ "$FRESH_INSTALL" = true ]; then
   838→    [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_HOST:-}" ] && die "SANDCASTLE_HOST is required (set in sandcastle.env)"
   839→
   840→    # When restoring from backup, admin credentials come from the backup DB —
   841→    # only SANDCASTLE_HOST (and TLS mode) are required from sandcastle.env.
   842→    if [ -z "$BACKUP_FILE" ]; then
   843→      [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL:-}" ] && die "SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL is required (set in sandcastle.env)"
   844→      [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}" ] && die "SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD is required (set in sandcastle.env or use SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE)"
   845→      [ ${#SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD} -lt 6 ] && die "SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD must be at least 6 characters"
   846→    fi
   847→
   848→    # SANDCASTLE_SUBNET no longer needed - networks allocated from dockyard pool
   849→
   850→    SECRET_KEY_BASE=$(openssl rand -hex 64)
   851→    DOCKER_GID=$(stat -c '%g' "$DOCKER_SOCK" 2>/dev/null || echo "988")
   852→
   853→    if [ -n "$BACKUP_FILE" ]; then
   854→      # ── Extract secrets from the backup file ──────────────────────────────
   855→      # Pull just the secrets files from the archive without extracting everything.
   856→      info "Extracting secrets from backup..."
   857→      local _bk_work
   858→      _bk_work=$(mktemp -d)
   859→      trap 'rm -rf "$_bk_work"' EXIT
   860→      tar --use-compress-program=zstd -xf "$BACKUP_FILE" -C "$_bk_work" \
   861→        sandcastle-backup/secrets/rails.secrets \
   862→        sandcastle-backup/secrets/postgres.secrets \
   863→        2>/dev/null \
   864→        || die "Cannot extract secrets from backup — is this a valid Sandcastle backup?"
   865→
   866→      # Load DB password from backup
   867→      POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres/.secrets"
   868→      mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres"
   869→      cp "$_bk_work/sandcastle-backup/secrets/postgres.secrets" "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   870→      chmod 600 "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   871→      # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   872→      source "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   873→      info "Using database password from backup"
   874→
   875→      # Load AR encryption keys from backup
   876→      RAILS_SECRETS_FILE="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails/.secrets"
   877→      mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails"
   878→      cp "$_bk_work/sandcastle-backup/secrets/rails.secrets" "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   879→      chmod 600 "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   880→      # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   881→      source "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   882→      info "Using AR encryption keys from backup"
   883→
   884→      rm -rf "$_bk_work"
   885→      trap - EXIT
   886→    else
   887→      # ── Normal fresh install: generate or reuse secrets ───────────────────
   888→
   889→      # Preserve DB password across reinstalls (user data)
   890→      POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres/.secrets"
   891→      if [ -f "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
   892→        # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   893→        source "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   894→        info "Reusing existing database password"
   895→      else
   896→        DB_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
   897→        mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/postgres"
   898→        echo "DB_PASSWORD=$DB_PASSWORD" > "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   899→        chmod 600 "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   900→        wrote "$POSTGRES_SECRETS_FILE"
   901→      fi
   902→
   903→      # Preserve AR encryption keys across reinstalls — required to decrypt
   904→      # encrypted settings (SMTP password, OAuth secrets) stored in the database.
   905→      # Each install gets unique keys; losing them makes encrypted values unrecoverable.
   906→      RAILS_SECRETS_FILE="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails/.secrets"
   907→      if [ -f "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
   908→        # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   909→        source "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   910→        info "Reusing existing AR encryption keys"
   911→      else
   912→        mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails"
   913→        AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
   914→        AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
   915→        AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
   916→        cat > "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE" <<SECRETS
   917→AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY=$AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY
   918→AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY=$AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY
   919→AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT=$AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT
   920→SECRETS
   921→        chmod 600 "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   922→        wrote "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   923→        warn "AR encryption keys generated. Back them up — losing them makes encrypted settings unrecoverable."
   924→      fi
   925→    fi
   926→
   927→    cat > "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" <<EOF
   928→# Sandcastle runtime — generated $(date -Iseconds)
   929→SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}"
   930→SANDCASTLE_NAME="${SANDCASTLE_NAME:-$(hostname -s 2>/dev/null || echo sandcastle)}"
   931→SANDCASTLE_HOST="${SANDCASTLE_HOST}"
   932→SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE}"
   933→SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER}"
   934→SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}"
   935→SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID}"
   936→SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID}"
   937→SECRET_KEY_BASE="${SECRET_KEY_BASE}"
   938→AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY="${AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY}"
   939→AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY="${AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY}"
   940→AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT="${AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT}"
   941→DB_PASSWORD="${DB_PASSWORD}"
   942→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER}"
   943→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL:-}"
   944→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}"
   945→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY:-}"
   946→DOCKER_GID="${DOCKER_GID}"
   947→DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKER_SOCK}"
   948→DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE}"
   949→ACME_EMAIL="${ACME_EMAIL:-}"
   950→GITHUB_CLIENT_ID="${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}"
   951→GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET="${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}"
   952→GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID="${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID:-}"
   953→GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET="${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET:-}"
   954→EOF
   955→    chmod 600 "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   956→    wrote "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   957→  else
   958→    info "Existing install — loading $SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   959→  fi
   960→
   961→  # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   962→  source "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   963→
   964→  # Backfill vars that may be missing in older .env files
   965→  if [ -z "${DOCKER_SOCK:-}" ]; then
   966→    DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker.sock"
   967→    echo "DOCKER_SOCK=$DOCKER_SOCK" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   968→  fi
   969→  # Backfill DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE — required so docker-compose passes the correct subnet to Rails
   970→  grep -q '^DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null || \
   971→    echo "DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE}" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   972→  # Backfill SANDCASTLE_NAME — used for Tailscale sidecar machine names (sc-<name>)
   973→  grep -q '^SANDCASTLE_NAME=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null || \
   974→    echo "SANDCASTLE_NAME=${SANDCASTLE_NAME:-$(hostname -s 2>/dev/null || echo sandcastle)}" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
   975→  # Backfill AR encryption keys — generated once, never rotated (losing them breaks encrypted DB values)
   976→  if ! grep -q '^AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null; then
   977→    RAILS_SECRETS_FILE="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails/.secrets"
   978→    if [ -f "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
   979→      # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   980→      source "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   981→    else
   982→      mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/rails"
   983→      AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
   984→      AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
   985→      AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
   986→      cat > "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE" <<SECRETS
   987→AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY=$AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY
   988→AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY=$AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY
   989→AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT=$AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT
   990→SECRETS
   991→      chmod 600 "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   992→      wrote "$RAILS_SECRETS_FILE"
   993→      warn "AR encryption keys generated. Back them up — losing them makes encrypted settings unrecoverable."
   994→    fi
   995→    cat >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" <<ARKEYS
   996→AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY=$AR_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY
   997→AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY=$AR_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY
   998→AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT=$AR_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT
   999→ARKEYS
  1000→    ok "AR encryption keys backfilled into .env"
  1001→  fi
  1002→  # Backfill OAuth vars from sandcastle.env into .env
  1003→  grep -q '^GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null || \
  1004→    echo "GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
  1005→  grep -q '^GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null || \
  1006→    echo "GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
  1007→  grep -q '^GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null || \
  1008→    echo "GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID:-}" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
  1009→  grep -q '^GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=' "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env" 2>/dev/null || \
  1010→    echo "GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET:-}" >> "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/.env"
  1011→
  1012→  # ── Write installed sandcastle.env ────────────────────────────────────────
  1013→
  1014→  cat > "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env" <<EOF
  1015→# Sandcastle installed configuration — generated $(date -Iseconds)
  1016→SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}"
  1017→APP_IMAGE="${APP_IMAGE}"
  1018→SANDBOX_IMAGE="${SANDBOX_IMAGE}"
  1019→SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER}"
  1020→SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP}"
  1021→SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID}"
  1022→SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID}"
  1023→SANDCASTLE_NAME="${SANDCASTLE_NAME:-$(hostname -s 2>/dev/null || echo sandcastle)}"
  1024→SANDCASTLE_HOST="${SANDCASTLE_HOST}"
  1025→SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE}"
  1026→SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT}"
  1027→SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT}"
  1028→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER}"
  1029→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL:-}"
  1030→SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY:-}"
  1031→ACME_EMAIL="${ACME_EMAIL:-}"
  1032→DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}"
  1033→DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}"
  1034→SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}"
  1035→DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR}"
  1036→DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR}"
  1037→DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE}"
  1038→DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE}"
  1039→DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKER_SOCK}"
  1040→DOCKER_GID="${DOCKER_GID:-988}"
  1041→GITHUB_CLIENT_ID="${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}"
  1042→GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET="${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}"
  1043→GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID="${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID:-}"
  1044→GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET="${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET:-}"
  1045→CREATED_USER="${CREATED_USER}"
  1046→CREATED_GROUP="${CREATED_GROUP}"
  1047→EOF
  1048→  chmod 600 "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env"
  1049→  wrote "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env"
  1050→
  1051→  # ── Traefik config ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1052→
  1053→  TRAEFIK_DIR="$SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik"
  1054→
  1055→  if [ "$SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE" = "selfsigned" ]; then
  1056→    if [ ! -f "$TRAEFIK_DIR/certs/cert.pem" ]; then
  1057→      info "Generating self-signed certificate..."
  1058→      openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 -sha256 -days 3650 -nodes \
  1059→        -keyout "$TRAEFIK_DIR/certs/key.pem" -out "$TRAEFIK_DIR/certs/cert.pem" \
  1060→        -subj "/CN=$SANDCASTLE_HOST" \
  1061→        -addext "subjectAltName=IP:$SANDCASTLE_HOST" 2>/dev/null
  1062→      ok "Self-signed certificate generated"
  1063→    fi
  1064→
  1065→    cat > "$TRAEFIK_DIR/traefik.yml" <<'TEOF'
  1066→entryPoints:
  1067→  web:
  1068→    address: ":80"
  1069→    http:
  1070→      redirections:
  1071→        entryPoint:
  1072→          to: websecure
  1073→          scheme: https
  1074→  websecure:
  1075→    address: ":443"
  1076→
  1077→providers:
  1078→  file:
  1079→    directory: /data/dynamic
  1080→    watch: true
  1081→
  1082→tls:
  1083→  certificates:
  1084→    - certFile: /data/certs/cert.pem
  1085→      keyFile: /data/certs/key.pem
  1086→
  1087→log:
  1088→  level: INFO
  1089→
  1090→api:
  1091→  dashboard: false
  1092→TEOF
  1093→
  1094→  elif [ "$SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE" = "mkcert" ]; then

1200→  routers:
  1201→    rails:
  1202→      rule: "Host(\`${SANDCASTLE_HOST}\`)"
  1203→      service: rails
  1204→      entryPoints:
  1205→        - websecure
  1206→      tls:
  1207→        certResolver: letsencrypt
  1208→  services:
  1209→    rails:
  1210→      loadBalancer:
  1211→        servers:
  1212→          - url: "http://sandcastle-web:80"
  1213→TEOF
  1214→  fi
  1215→  wrote "$TRAEFIK_DIR/dynamic/rails.yml"
  1216→
  1217→  chown "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" "$TRAEFIK_DIR/traefik.yml"
  1218→  chown -R "${SANDCASTLE_UID}:${SANDCASTLE_GID}" "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"/data/traefik/dynamic
  1219→
  1220→  # ── Docker network ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1221→
  1222→  # ── Create sandcastle-web network (allocated from dockyard pool) ────────────
  1223→  # Let dockyard allocate the subnet from its address pool (DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE)
  1224→  # so dockyard's own iptables rules handle NAT/forwarding automatically.
  1225→  # No separate sandcastle-nat service needed.
  1226→  if $DOCKER network inspect sandcastle-web &>/dev/null; then
  1227→    ok "sandcastle-web network exists"
  1228→  else
  1229→    $DOCKER network create sandcastle-web >/dev/null
  1230→    ok "sandcastle-web network created"
  1231→  fi
  1232→
  1233→  # ── Pull images ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1234→
  1235→  info "Pulling images..."
  1236→  $DOCKER pull "$APP_IMAGE" &
  1237→  $DOCKER pull "$SANDBOX_IMAGE" &
  1238→  $DOCKER pull traefik:v3.3 &
  1239→  wait
  1240→  ok "Images pulled"
  1241→
  1242→  # ── Write PostgreSQL init script ──────────────────────────────────────────
  1243→
  1244→  mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/postgres"
  1245→  cat > "$REDACTED.sh" <<'INITDB'
  1246→@@TEMPLATE:/docker/postgres/init-databases.sh@@
  1247→INITDB
  1248→  chmod +x "$REDACTED.sh"
  1249→  wrote "$REDACTED.sh"
  1250→
  1251→  write_compose
  1252→
  1253→  write_helper_scripts
  1254→
  1255→  # ── Install prerequisites and sandcastle-admin ────────────────────────────
  1256→
  1257→  install_prerequisites
  1258→  write_admin_script
  1259→
  1260→  # ── Start services ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1261→
  1262→  cd "$SANDCASTLE_HOME"
  1263→
  1264→  if [ "$FRESH_INSTALL" = true ] && [ -n "$BACKUP_FILE" ]; then
  1265→    # ── Restore from backup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1266→    # Start postgres first, restore data, then bring up everything.
  1267→
  1268→    info "Starting PostgreSQL for restore..."
  1269→    $DOCKER compose up -d postgres
  1270→    local _i
  1271→    for _i in $(seq 1 30); do
  1272→      $DOCKER compose exec -T postgres \
  1273→        pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production &>/dev/null && break
  1274→      sleep 2
  1275→    done
  1276→    ok "PostgreSQL ready"
  1277→
  1278→    # Extract and restore from the backup (db + data + images).
  1279→    # Use sandcastle-admin restore for the actual restore logic.
  1280→    info "Restoring from backup: $BACKUP_FILE"
  1281→    SANDCASTLE_HOME="$SANDCASTLE_HOME" \
  1282→      "$REDACTED" restore "$BACKUP_FILE" \
  1283→      --yes --skip-images 2>/dev/null \
  1284→      || die "Restore failed — check the backup file and try again"
  1285→
  1286→    # Load snapshot images (docker save/load requires the daemon to be running)
  1287→    # The restore command skipped them above; load manually now if present.
  1288→    info "Starting Sandcastle..."
  1289→    $DOCKER compose up -d
  1290→
  1291→    # Load snapshot images now that the Dockyard daemon is fully up
  1292→    local _tmp_bk
  1293→    _tmp_bk=$(mktemp -d)
  1294→    tar --use-compress-program=zstd -xf "$BACKUP_FILE" -C "$_tmp_bk" \
  1295→      sandcastle-backup/images 2>/dev/null || true
  1296→    if [ -d "$_tmp_bk/sandcastle-backup/images" ]; then
  1297→      info "Loading snapshot Docker images..."
  1298→      local _img
  1299→      for _img in "$_tmp_bk/sandcastle-backup/images/"*.tar; do
  1300→        [ -f "$_img" ] || continue
  1301→        info "  Loading $(basename "$_img")..."
  1302→        $DOCKER load < "$_img" && ok "  $(basename "$_img")"
  1303→      done
  1304→    fi
  1305→    rm -rf "$_tmp_bk"
  1306→  else
  1307→    info "Starting Sandcastle..."
  1308→    $DOCKER compose up -d
  1309→
  1310→    # ── Seed database (fresh install without backup) ──────────────────────
  1311→
  1312→    if [ "$FRESH_INSTALL" = true ]; then
  1313→      info "Waiting for app to be ready..."
  1314→      for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  1315→        if curl -sfk https://localhost:${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT}/up >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  1316→          break
  1317→        fi
  1318→        sleep 2
  1319→      done
  1320→
  1321→      info "Seeding database..."
  1322→      $DOCKER compose exec -T \
  1323→        -e SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER}" \
  1324→        -e SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL}" \
  1325→        -e SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD}" \
  1326→        -e SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY:-}" \
  1327→        web ./bin/rails db:seed
  1328→    fi
  1329→  fi
  1330→
  1331→  # ── Done ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1332→
  1333→  # Build base URL with port suffix only when non-standard
  1334→  local port_suffix=""
  1335→  [ "$SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT" != "443" ] && port_suffix=":${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT}"
